cards.title: Core Concepts
cards.content:
• Components of Premium
• Net Premium vs. Gross Premium (Formulas)
• Valuation and Surplus (Actuary's Role)
• Types of Bonuses

cards.title: 1. Components
cards.content:
Mortality: Cost of death risk.
Interest: Discount from investment earnings.
Expenses: Cost of running business.

cards.title: 2. The Formulas (Must Remember!)
cards.content:
Net Premium = Mortality - Interest
Gross Premium = Net Premium + Expenses

cards.title: 3. The Actuary's Role
cards.content: An 'Actuary' is the specialist who calculates premiums and conducts valuation (comparing Assets vs Liabilities).

cards.title: 4. Surplus & Bonus
cards.content: Surplus = Assets - Liabilities. This surplus is given to 'Participating' policyholders as a Bonus.

cards.title: Allocation Methods
cards.content:
• Simple Reversionary: % of Sum Assured only.
• Compound Reversionary: % of (Sum Assured + Previous Bonuses).
• Terminal Bonus: Paid only when policy ends.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: Gross Premium formula: Net Premium + Expenses.
Surplus definition: Excess of Assets over Liabilities.
Interest effect: Always reduces the premium amount.
